beats.narration: Penetrant finds a flaw only when it can get inside it. Before any penetrant touches the part, two conditions must already be true.

payload.title: PREPARATION & LIGHTING
payload.sub: Clean, open, dry — then verify the light.

beats.narration: Capillary action can only pull penetrant into an opening that is actually open. Three contamination families defeat it: soils, solid barriers, and smeared metal.

payload.heading: What seals a flaw before the test starts

beats.narration: Match the cleaning method to the contaminant. Then two rules protect the test: rinse residues away, and dry the part before penetrant goes on.

payload.heading: Clean to the contaminant, then dry

beats.narration: Now lighting, verified before processing, measured at the surface. Visible dye needs strong white light. Fluorescent flips it: intense UV and a darkened booth.
Lock in the gate. Clean surface, open flaws, dry part, verified light. Get any one wrong and the rest of the process is theater.

payload.sub: The foundation the whole penetrant process stands on.
